key_insight: SimPhysNet uses physics-informed self-supervised learning plus few-shot prototypical classification to predict laser welding penetration with high accuracy from very few labeled images.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the labeling burden for industrial welding inspection while supporting more reliable, automated control of weld quality.
method: Physics-informed neural network (PINN) guides feature learning from abundant unlabeled molten-pool/keyhole images.
Contrastive self-supervised learning is strengthened with three image augmentation tasks.
Prototypical networks perform few-shot classification using a small labeled set.
evidence_signal: The model achieved 96.06% classification accuracy using only 200 labeled images, about 5% of the labeled dataset, and matched conventional supervised methods trained on all labels.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training data and physical priors match the specific welding setup, material, and imaging conditions.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
